name: Refrigerator Wheat Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. Soften the yeast in lukewarm water; set aside.
2. Scald the milk, add honey and salt, then cool to lukewarm.
3. Add 2 cups whole wheat flour and beat well.
4. Add the yeast mixture, eggs, and shortening; blend well.
5. Add the remaining whole wheat flour and white flour to make a soft dough.
6. Knead until smooth and satiny.
7. Place in a lightly greased bowl and grease the top of the dough.
8. Cover well and put into the refrigerator.
9. After no less than 12 hours or more than 4 days, remove from the refrigerator and punch down.
10. Shape the rolls as desired and place them in non-stick pans.
11. Let rise until doubled in bulk.
12. Bake at 425 degrees F for 15-20 minutes.
(113 calories per serving) 1 serving=1 Bread exchange 1 Fat exchange
